title: Substrate Project Configuration
description: Schema for `substrate.config.json` at the consumer repo root. Mirrors the runtime SubstrateConfig type. Additive: v2 fields (e.g. memory.path) are optional so older configs continue to validate.

version.description: Substrate config schema version this file was written against.

memory.description: v2.0 memory subsystem configuration. Optional; when omitted, substrate falls back to $SUBSTRATE_MEMORY_PATH then Claude Code's default directory (~/.claude/projects/<encoded>/memory/). See plan §6.1.

path.description: Absolute or repo-relative path to the memory store directory. Memories are expected to be `*.md` files with optional YAML frontmatter; see plan §6.2 for the extended frontmatter shape.

extends.description: v3.0 org-scoped content composition (NE-11). Each entry declares an upstream substrate-content source. The resolver walks each source's substrate/ tree and merges per-kind. Repo-local content always wins on collision. Entries are evaluated in order: earlier entries are the base; later entries override earlier; the repo's own substrate/ overrides all. See substrate-phase-1-2-enterprise-plan.md §2.

source.description: Source URL. One of: 'npm:<pkg-name>' (resolved via node_modules), 'github:<org>/<repo>' (cloned to substrate/.cache/extends/), or 'file:<relative-path>' (live filesystem reference).

version.description: Optional npm semver range. Only meaningful for 'npm:' sources; ignored otherwise. Defaults to whatever the consumer's node_modules has installed.

ref.description: Optional git ref (tag, branch, or commit SHA). Only meaningful for 'github:' sources; ignored otherwise. Defaults to the source repo's default branch (resolved at clone time).
